macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Mixed Amid Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: Most U.S. stocks fell on Wednesday with the S&P 500 down 0.1-0.3%, Dow dropping 0.6-0.8%, and Nasdaq up 0.1%, following volatility from the war with Iran. Oil prices climbed despite IEA members releasing record crude stockpiles, while Treasury yields rose.[1]

items.title: Oracle Jumps on Strong Earnings, Limits Wall Street Losses
items.summary: Oracle surged 8.3% after reporting stronger-than-expected profit and revenue, raising its revenue growth forecast due to AI cloud computing demand. This helped cap broader market declines amid falling stocks.[1]

items.title: Campbell’s Sinks After Weak Earnings Report
items.summary: Campbell’s shares dropped 6.7% following a weaker-than-expected quarterly profit, impacted by struggles in its snack business, leading to cut forecasts for revenue and profit this fiscal year.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: BlackRock Prepares Massive Move into XRP Tokenization Amid Liquidity Crisis
items.summary: BlackRock is reportedly gearing up for tokenization of securities on the XRP Ledger as the $2 trillion private credit market faces a liquidity trap in March 2026. Over $2.3 billion in real-world assets have already migrated to the chain, with 2026 roadmap including XLS-66 lending and EVM sidechain.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $70,000 Amid Oil Prices and Inflation Watch
items.summary: Bitcoin is testing the $70,000 level as investors monitor oil prices, inflation data, and mixed signals on the Iran conflict. Market reports show BTC around $69,300-$70,000 with key resistance levels in focus.

items.title: Binance Sues Wall Street Journal Over Defamation Claims
items.summary: Binance has filed a lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal alleging defamation. This comes amid broader crypto market updates including federal regulator guidance on tokenized securities.

items.title: XRP Trades at $1.37 as Markets Eye US CPI Data
items.summary: XRP is priced at $1.37 in morning market updates, with Bitcoin at $69,300. Upcoming US CPI data could influence crypto sentiment and break key resistances.

items.title: Federal Regulators Issue New Guidance on Tokenized Securities
items.summary: Securitize's Carlos Domingo discusses new federal guidance impacting tokenized securities. This regulatory clarity is highlighted as a key development for blockchain adoption.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: ThreatLabz 2026 AI Security Report Reveals Rapid Enterprise AI Adoption and Expanding Risks
items.summary: Enterprise AI/ML transactions surged 83% year-over-year in 2025, with over 3,400 applications now generating traffic, significantly widening the attack surface. Threat actors are using generative AI for social engineering, evasion, and malware, complicating detection.

items.title: AI Autocomplete Features Subtly Influence Users' Views on Social Issues, Study Finds
items.summary: Research published March 11 in Science Advances shows that biased AI autocomplete suggestions shift opinions on topics like the death penalty, even if users reject them, with participants unaware of the manipulation.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Anthropic Institute to Study AI's Societal and Economic Impacts
items.summary: Anthropic announced the Anthropic Institute, led by co-founder Jack Clark, uniting teams like the Frontier Red Team to address AI challenges, amid recent tensions with the US DoD over ethics leading to a Pentagon ban.

items.title: Datavault AI CEO to Present Tokenized Legacy Solution for Hollywood AI Concerns at Luminary 2026
items.summary: Datavault AI's platform addresses 2023 Hollywood strikes' AI issues by ensuring consent, on-chain royalties, and immutable rights for digital replicas and NIL usage, with CEO presenting on March 14.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Garry Newman Announces s&box Pricing Similar to Garry's Mod
items.summary: Garry Newman states his game creation platform s&box will likely be priced similarly to Garry's Mod, which he originally expected to earn only $30,000 in a best-case scenario.

items.title: Sega Addresses $200 Million Rovio Impairment Write-Down
items.summary: Sega attributes a $200 million impairment on its $776 million Rovio acquisition to overreliance on local management and challenges integrating Rovio's Beacon system into its mobile games, pledging marketing and sales reforms.

items.title: Boss Fight Entertainment Revived After Netflix Closure
items.summary: The studio behind Squid Game: Unleashed, closed by Netflix six months ago, has regrouped as Bossfight and is developing a new PC and mobile game.

items.title: Highguard Free-to-Play Shooter Shutting Down March 12
items.summary: Wildlight Entertainment's hero shooter Highguard, released earlier this year, will close on March 12 due to failure to build a sustainable player base.

items.title: For Honor Launches Year 10 Cycle of War on March 12
items.summary: Ubisoft's For Honor celebrates its 10th year with the Cycle of War launch on March 12, including hero balancing updates, free Gryphon trials, and continued support beyond Year 10.

items.title: PS Plus Extra and Premium March 2026 Games Confirmed
items.summary: Sony has officially revealed the full list of games joining PS Plus Extra and Premium for March 2026 following an earlier leak.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Oracle Shares Surge 14% After Strong Q3 Earnings and 2027 Outlook
items.summary: Oracle beat Q3 estimates and provided an upbeat 2027 sales outlook, boosting shares by 14% and lifting the software sector despite year-to-date declines.

items.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in Nebius for Data Center Development
items.summary: Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Nebius to develop data centers, sending Nebius shares up 14.6% amid efforts to diversify beyond major hyperscalers.

items.title: Synopsys Launches Ansys 2026 R1 with Advanced Simulation Tools
items.summary: Synopsys released Ansys 2026 R1 at Synopsys Converge, featuring HFSS-PI for broadband 3D power integrity simulation to address chip-package integration challenges.

items.title: Chip Stocks Rise: Micron and AMD Gain 3% Each in Mixed Market
items.summary: Micron Tech and AMD shares each rose about 3% as chip stocks stood out positively amid improving tech sentiment, while oil prices edged higher.

items.title: Synopsys Introduces Software-Defined Hardware-Assisted Verification
items.summary: Synopsys unveiled software-defined hardware-assisted verification solutions at Synopsys Converge to support advanced chip design for the AI era.
